---
title: "Prediction-Based Inference Short Course"
subtitle: "Methods & Applications"
date: 2026-03-15T13:00:00-07:00
date-format: "dddd, MMMM D, YYYY | h:mm a z"
toc: true
toc-location: right
author: 
  - name: Tyler H. McCormick
date-title: "Time"
listing:
  - id: core_units
    contents: 
      - content/Unit00_GettingStarted.qmd
      - content/Unit02_MeasuringAdiposity.qmd
      - content/Unit01_AlphaFold.qmd
      - content/Unit05_RashomonQuartet.qmd
    type: grid
    sort: "unit"
    filter-ui: false
    fields: [title, image, subtitle, description]
  - id: supplemental_units
    contents:
      - content/Unit03_GeneticData.qmd
    type: grid
    sort: "unit"
    filter-ui: false
    fields: [title, image, subtitle, description]
---

::: {.landing-page}

::: {.hero-grid}

::: {.hero-diagram}
![Source: [https://arxiv.org/pdf/2411.19908](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2411.19908)](content/images/pbinference_gronsbell.PNG)
:::

::: {.goals-card}
### Workshop Goals and Objectives

**Learning Goals**:

* **Understand** limitations in using predicted data for inference.
* **Learn** about methods that correct for bias and recover valid uncertainty estimates.
* **Gain** practical skills using the [`ipd`](https://github.com/ipd-tools/ipd) R package.

**Learning Objectives**:

* **Explore** data with AI/ML-predicted outcomes and diagnose bias/variance in predictions.
* **Apply** `ipd::ipd()` to continuous and binary outcomes.
* **Interpret** prediction-based (PB) inference outputs and visualize model results.
:::

:::

### Time Outline (4 hours)

| Activity                                                              | Time |
| --------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---- |
| [Overview & Introductions](content/slides.qmd)                        | 40 m |
| Short Break                                                           | 5 m  |
| [Getting Started](content/Unit00_GettingStarted.qmd)                  | 30 m |
| Break                                                                 | 20 m |
| [Module 1: Measuring Adiposity](content/Unit02_MeasuringAdiposity.qmd)| 30 m |
| Short Break                                                           | 5 m  |
| [Module 2: Proteomics with AlphaFold](content/Unit01_AlphaFold.qmd)   | 30 m |
| Break                                                                 | 20 m |
| [Module 3: Rashomon Quartet](content/Unit05_RashomonQuartet.qmd)      | 40 m |
| [Wrap-Up, Conclusions, and Questions](content/wrap_up.qmd)            | 20 m |

::: {.quickstart-block}
## Quick Start

The companion website for this workshop is available at: 

[https://thmccormick.github.io/ipd-short-course](https://thmccormick.github.io/ipd-short-course)

To use the workshop image:

```sh
docker run -e PASSWORD=<choose_a_password_for_rstudio> -p 8787:8787 ghcr.io/thmccormick/ipd-short-course:latest
```

Once running, navigate to `http://localhost:8787/` and then log in with `rstudio`:`yourchosenpassword`. 

Then [begin](content/Unit00_GettingStarted.qmd)!
:::

## Short Course Overview

In this short course, we explore the consequences of conducting inference on 
predicted data across several applications and present a suite of 
prediction-based (PB) inference methods that adjust for prediction-related 
uncertainty to improve inference validity and efficiency. We also introduce [`ipd`](https://github.com/ipd-tools/ipd), a user-friendly `R` package that 
implements the PB inference methods through a unified interface. The package 
supports modular integration into existing workflows and includes 
[`tidy`](https://broom.tidymodels.org/index.html) methods for model inspection 
and diagnostics.

## Modules

This short course covers four modules, each illustrated with the 
[`ipd`](https://github.com/ipd-tools/ipd) package:[^cover-images]

:::{#core_units}
:::

## Supplemental Module

We have also included an additional module for self-guided exploration:

:::{#supplemental_units}
:::

## Participation

This short course uses a blended format of **instruction** and 
**hands-on coding exercises**. Participants should:

* Follow along in the virtual RStudio environment (see below).
* Attempt to complete brief exercises or run the solution code snippets in real time.
* Engage in Q&A at module boundaries to troubleshoot and discuss concepts.

## Prerequisites

* A computer with internet access.
* Familiarity with base `R` and `tidyverse` syntax (e.g., `dplyr`, `broom`).
* Basic understanding of predictive (e.g., `randomForest`) and regression modeling (e.g., `lm`, `glm`).
* Optional: Exposure to Bioconductor's `ExpressionSet`, `AnnotationDbi`, and `MLInterfaces` is helpful for one of the supplemental modules.

## Instructor and Acknowledgement

**Presenter:**
Tyler H. McCormick [&#x2709;&#xFE0E;](mailto:thmccormick@gmail.com)

**Acknowledgement:**
This short course adapts and extends Stephen Salerno's original IPD workshop website and materials.

**Original Workshop Contributors (Alphabetical Order):**
Awan Afiaz [&#x2709;&#xFE0E;](mailto:aafiaz@uw.edu), David Cheng [&#x2709;&#xFE0E;](mailto:dcheng@mgh.harvard.edu), Jianhui Gao [&#x2709;&#xFE0E;](mailto:jianhui.gao@mail.utoronto.ca), Jesse Gronsbell [&#x2709;&#xFE0E;](mailto:j.gronsbell@utoronto.ca), Kentaro Hoffman [&#x2709;&#xFE0E;](mailto:khoffm3@uw.edu), Jeff Leek [&#x2709;&#xFE0E;](mailto:jtleek@fredhutch.org), Qiongshi Lu [&#x2709;&#xFE0E;](mailto:qlu@biostat.wisc.edu), Tyler McCormick [&#x2709;&#xFE0E;](mailto:tylermc@uw.edu), Jiacheng Miao [&#x2709;&#xFE0E;](mailto:jmiao24@wisc.edu), Anna Neufeld [&#x2709;&#xFE0E;](mailto:acn2@williams.edu), Stephen Salerno [&#x2709;&#xFE0E;](mailto:ssalerno@fredhutch.org)

[^cover-images]: Module card cover images were generated by GPT-5.2.

:::
